claims.claim_text: Building the orchestration, retrieval, parsing, and action layers for production LLM apps from scratch takes months.
claims.anchor_text: Writing this from scratch takes months.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Building this from scratch is usually a substantial engineering effort.
claims.rationale: The exact duration is uncited and highly context-dependent.

claims.claim_text: LangChain is a framework for building agents and LLM-powered applications, with standard interfaces for models, embeddings, vector stores, and integrations.
claims.rationale: The LangChain README directly describes the framework this way.

claims.claim_text: LlamaIndex is a data-centric framework for LLM applications, with connectors for sources such as APIs, PDFs, docs, and SQL plus retrieval/query tooling.
claims.rationale: The LlamaIndex README directly describes these capabilities.

claims.anchor_text: - LangChain has **600+ integrations** with LLMs, vector stores, tools, and APIs
claims.suggested_rewrite: - LangChain has **hundreds of integrations** with LLMs, vector stores, tools, and APIs
claims.rationale: The exact count is a moving target and needs a live source that states that number verbatim.

claims.anchor_text: - The RAG pattern (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) can **reduce hallucinations by 80%+**
claims.suggested_rewrite: - The RAG pattern (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) can **substantially reduce hallucinations by grounding answers in retrieved context**
claims.rationale: A single percentage is too specific for a claim that varies widely by model, dataset, and evaluation method.

claims.claim_text: LangChain's agent framework powers many AI assistants used daily.
claims.anchor_text: - LangChain's agent framework powers many of the "AI assistants" you use daily
claims.suggested_rewrite: - LangChain provides agent-building patterns used in many assistant-style applications
claims.rationale: This is a sweeping adoption claim without an attributable primary source.

claims.claim_text: LlamaIndex can index structured data, code, and SQL databases, not just documents.
claims.rationale: Official LlamaIndex docs surface connectors and use cases such as code and Text-to-SQL, but the docs host is off allowlist.

claims.claim_text: LangChain has official local-model integrations such as Ollama.
claims.rationale: Official LangChain docs directly cover Ollama integration, but the docs host is off allowlist.

claims.claim_text: LlamaIndex supports both OpenAI and non-OpenAI LLM backends such as Ollama.
claims.span_hint: section: Did You Know, bullet 6 / section: LlamaIndex Fundamentals
claims.rationale: The README shows both OpenAI usage and non-OpenAI usage via Ollama.

claims.claim_text: A good default is 10-20% chunk overlap in RAG systems.
claims.span_hint: section: Common Mistakes, row 'No chunk overlap'
claims.anchor_text: | No chunk overlap | Missing context at boundaries | Use 10-20% overlap |
claims.suggested_rewrite: | No chunk overlap | Context can be lost at boundaries | Use a modest overlap and tune it with retrieval tests |
claims.rationale: Chunk overlap is a valid lesson, but the 10-20% figure is a heuristic rather than a universal constant.

claims.claim_text: Typical RAG chunk sizes are 500-1000 tokens.
claims.span_hint: section: Common Mistakes, row 'Chunks too large'
claims.anchor_text: | Chunks too large | Diluted relevance | 500-1000 tokens typical |
claims.suggested_rewrite: | Chunks too large | Retrieval can become less focused | Start with moderate chunk sizes and tune them for your corpus |
claims.rationale: Chunk-size guidance is workload-specific, so the exact range should be presented as tunable guidance rather than a hard default.

claims.claim_text: Prompt injection is a security vulnerability in LLM applications.
claims.rationale: OWASP directly documents prompt injection as an LLM security vulnerability and describes mitigations.

claims.claim_text: A fintech company had a real production failure where a LangChain customer-support chatbot lost conversational context after deployment.
claims.anchor_text: A fintech company built a customer support chatbot using LangChain. It worked great in demos but failed in production.
claims.suggested_rewrite: A common production failure mode for LLM chatbots is losing conversational context between requests, even when demos look strong.
claims.rationale: This is an unattributed war story presented as a real incident.

claims.claim_text: Customer satisfaction improved from 45% to 82% after the memory fix.
claims.suggested_rewrite: - Customer satisfaction can improve markedly when follow-up context is handled correctly
claims.rationale: These exact before/after metrics are unsourced incident specifics.

claims.claim_text: Issue resolution rate improved from 30% to 65% after the memory fix.
claims.suggested_rewrite: - Issue resolution rates often improve when the assistant can use prior turns
claims.rationale: These exact before/after metrics are unsourced incident specifics.

claims.claim_text: Average conversation length increased from 2 messages to 8 messages after the memory fix.
claims.suggested_rewrite: - Conversations often become more useful when the assistant can maintain context across turns
claims.rationale: These exact before/after metrics are unsourced incident specifics.

further_reading.title: LangChain README
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary upstream overview of LangChain's architecture, ecosystem, and core capabilities.

further_reading.title: LlamaIndex README
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary upstream overview of LlamaIndex's data connectors, retrieval workflow, and backend integrations.

further_reading.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Large Language Models: A Survey
further_reading.why_relevant: Good survey source for RAG concepts, retrieval design tradeoffs, and evaluation framing.

notes: Code blocks, quiz items, exercise steps, and the Mermaid-style diagram were skipped per instructions. The Key Takeaways section repeats earlier chunking/local-model claims; if the injector cites summaries separately, reuse the same sources or rewrites from the earlier claims.
